metadata.composer: 'Consolator (Webbe)' Samuel Webbe, 1792 "The Church Hymnal, Revised and Enlarged" (Episcopal), 1896
metadata.lyricist: verses 1-2 Thomas Moore, 1816.  Verse 3 Thomas Hastings, 1831

c_lines: Words: verses 1-2 Thomas Moore, 1816.  Verse 3 Thomas Hastings, 1831.
Setting: "The Church Hymnal, Revised and Enlarged" (Episcopal), 1896.
copyright: public domain.  This score is a part of the Open Hymnal Project, 2013 Revision.

1.raw_text: Come, ye disconsolate, wher'er ye languish; Come to the mercy seat, fervently kneel. Here bring your wounded hearts, here tell your anguish; Earth has no sorrow that Heav'n cannot heal.

2.raw_text: Joy of the deso- late, light of the straying, Hope of the peni- tent, fadeless and pure, Here speaks the Comforter, tenderly saying, "Earth has no sorrow that Heav'n cannot cure."

3.raw_text: Here see the Bread of Life; see waters flowing Forth from the throne of God, pure from above; Come to the feast of love; come, ever knowing Earth has no sorrow but Heav'n can remove.
